evals.prompt: I want to add a new fire-school ring spell that behaves like an existing fire spell but with different range and damage numbers. What kind of work is this?
evals.expected_output: Classifies the ask as pattern reuse and keeps the route on the ring-spell authoring path without inventing unnecessary mechanic scope.
expectations: Classifies the ask as pattern reuse or the equivalent low-scope bucket
Routes to adding-spell or ring-spell authoring
Does not invent new-school platform work

evals.prompt: Add a new ring spell that needs a brand-new projectile visual and impact burst. What else do I need beyond the spell file?
evals.expected_output: Classifies the ask as animation-heavy work and routes to both spell authoring and animation ownership surfaces.
expectations: Classifies the ask as new status or animation work
Routes to adding-animation in addition to adding-spell
Names animation refs and web-rendering surfaces

evals.prompt: I want a combo spell that requires mastery in two schools before study unlocks. Is this still just ring-spell content?
evals.expected_output: Rejects content-only scope, classifies the ask as platform expansion, and calls out presenter/UI or study-surface limitations.
expectations: Classifies the ask as new-school or combo expansion
Says the work is not content-only
Names presenter, game-view, or ring-spell availability surfaces

evals.prompt: I want to add a brand-new frost ring school and a spell package for it. What buckets does that trip?
evals.expected_output: Treats the ask as new-school platform work, names school files plus downstream runtime and contract implications, and routes to ring plus mechanic workflows.
expectations: Classifies the ask as new-school expansion
Calls out runtime or contract implications beyond content
